title: lodhra loha
intro: Lodhra loha, also known as Symplocos racemosa, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region of Asia, commonly found in the mountainous regions of India, Nepal, and Bhutan. The medicinal properties of Lodhra loha plant are attributed to its bark, leaves, and flowers, which are rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of using Lodhra loha include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which help in treating various health conditions such as menstrual disorders, digestive issues, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Lodhra loha include its bark powder, decoction, and oil, which can be used as a topical application or internal medication to harness its medicinal properties.
